properties.description:en: Seat of the mayor and city council of the city of Vienna andgovernor and assembly of the State of Vienna. Neo-gothic building. (Source: Wikipedia:en)
properties.name: Rathaus

properties.description:en: 39 exhibit halls include the world’s largest and oldest public collection of meteorites, a new permanent anthropological exhibition on the origins and development of humans, and a Digital Planetarium. (Source: official website)
properties.name: Naturhistorisches Museum
properties.name:es: Museo de Historia Natural de Viena

properties.description:en: Museum of art history main collections: classic western paintings, scuplture, coins, egytptian, greek/roman.
properties.name: Kunsthistorisches Museum

properties.description: Austrian national parliament building. Neoclassical style. (Source: Wikipedia:de)
properties.name: Parlament
properties.wikimedia_commons: Category:Exterior of the Austrian Parliament Building

properties.description:en: The longest single residential building in the world, 1100m. Gemeindebauten (English: municipal tenement complexes). Played a role in 1934 Austrian Civil War. (Source: Wikipedia:en)
properties.name: Karl-Marx-Hof

properties.description:en: Furniture of the Habsburg monarchs + an overview of the history of Viennese cabinet making and interior decoration. (Source: Wikipedia:en)
properties.name: Möbel Museum Wien

properties.description:en: Baroque palace built for garrison commander Count Wirich Philipp von Daun. In 1784 it was sold tothe Bohemian Kinsky family. Currently used for auction events, houses shops and a restaurant. (Source: wikipedia:en)
properties.name: Palais Kinsky

properties.description:en: High-Baroque palace built for Prince Eugene of Savoy
properties.name: Winterpalais

properties.description:en: Former imperial residence: 1438 - 1583 and 1612 -1806, it was the seat of the kings and emperors of the Holy Roman Empire of the German Nation, thereafter the seat of the Emperor of Austria until 1918. Today it is the official seat of the Austrian Federal
properties.name: Hofburg

properties.description:en: Austrian military history since 1600s.
properties.name: Heeresgeschichtliches Museum

properties.name: Hofjagd- und Rüstkammer

properties.description:en: The shorter tour of the Schönbrunn palace.
properties.name: Imperial Tour

properties.description:en: The longer tour of the Schönbrunn palace.

properties.description:en: Baroque palace owned by the princely Schwarzenberg family. Completed in 1728. Source: Wikipedia:en

properties.description:en: Building. Built as the Austrian National Bank building plus trading floor.

properties.description:en: History of the city. Main building of the Vienna Museum. The group includes numerous specialised museums, musicians' residences and archaeological excavations.
properties.name: Wien Museum Karlsplatz
properties.note:height: Höhe bezieht sich auf Höhe der Aussichtsplatform

properties.description:en: "MUseum MOderner Kunst". 10,000 modern and contemporary art works, including major works from Andy Warhol, Pablo Picasso, Joseph Beuys, Nam June Paik, Wolf Vostell, Gerhard Richter, Jasper Johns and Roy Lichtenstein. (Source: Wikipedia:en)
properties.name: Museum Moderner Kunst Stiftung Ludwig Wien

properties.description:en: World-famous opera house in Neo-Renaissance style.
properties.name: Wiener Staatsoper
properties.wikimedia_commons: Category:Exterior of Vienna State Opera House

properties.description:en: Former imperial summer residence, the current main building dates from 1740-50s remodelling. Source: Wikipedia:en
properties.name: Schloss Schönbrunn

properties.description:en: Replica of the ancient Theseion in Athens and housed Antonio Canova's sculpture Theseus group. Used by Kunsthistorisches Museum for exhibitions.
properties.name: Theseustempel

properties.description:en: Neo-Gothic church. Built after attempted assassination of Emperor Franz Joseph in 1853at his brother's iniiative to give thanks for sparing his life. (Source: Wikipedia:en)
properties.name: Votivkirche

properties.architect: Johann Bernhard Fischer von Erlach;Johann Lucas von Hildebrandt;Anton Ospel;Antonio Beduzzi;Nicolò Pacassi
properties.description:en: Baroque palace of the imperial Habsburg family.
properties.name: Schloss Hetzendorf

properties.description:en: Largest and best known example of a "gloriette", a building in a garden deliberately built elevated with respect to the surroundings. Source: Wikipedia:en
properties.name: Gloriette

properties.description:en: Vienna landmark building. Art Nouveau exhibition hall built by a group of artists, including Gustav Klimt, who broke away from the traditionalist Association of Austrian Artists to form the Vienna Secession movement. (Source: survey)
properties.name: Secession

properties.description: Home of the Vienna Philharmonic orchestra.. The building was inaugurated on January 6, 1870. It's most famous concert is the New Years Concert broadcast live around the world on New Years Day.
properties.name: Musikverein
properties.name:zh: 愛樂協會大樓金色大厅

properties.description:en: Artist-owned building. Exhibitions focus on contemporary art, architecture, design, media, film, interdisciplinary theme-exhibitions, international cooperations as well as exhibitions by members.
properties.name: Künstlerhaus Wien
properties.operator: der Gesellschaft bildender Künstlerinnen und Künstler Österreichs

properties.description:en: Rose garden inside Wettsteinpark, 317 varieties of roses in 1,300 sq.m. (Source: Wikipedia:es)
properties.name: Rosarium Wettsteinpark

properties.description:en: Ceremonial outer castle gate on the site of the former military gate blown up by Napoleanic troops 1809. (Source: wikipedia:de)
properties.name: Burgtor

properties.description:en: Ornate water-tower built in industrial historicism style. Internal access by appointment or during event.
properties.name: Wasserturm Favoriten
Hilton Vienna Waterfront
properties.wheelchair:description: Rollstuhlfahrer parken vor der Tür am Haupteingang. Aus -1 bei den normalen Parkplätzen kein Zugang möglich. Treppen!

properties.description:en: Vienna's most popular food market. Originated in C16th. About 1.5km long. Open air with permanent stalls and many cafes. Open every day. (Main source: wikipedia:en)
properties.name: Naschmarkt

properties.description:en: Originally a winter garden that could be heated, then stables, now an art exhibition space.
properties.name: Orangerie

properties.description:en: Tallest structure in Austria and one of the highest 75 towers in the world. Observation deck, restaurant, café. (Source: website, wikipedia:en)
properties.name: Donauturm
Spark by Hilton Vienna Messe Prater

properties.description:en: Large statue of Empress Maria Theresa with supporting figures.
properties.name: Maria-Theresien-Denkmal

properties.description:de: Das Hotel Odeon befindet sich im 2.Wiener Gemeindebezirk, unweit sowohl von Wiens berühmten historischen Stadtkern als auch von der Messe Wien.
properties.name: Hotel Odeon

properties.architect: Walter Bassett Basset;Harry Hitchins;Hubert Cecil Booth
properties.name: Riesenrad
properties.operator: Wiener Riesenrad Dr. Lamac GmbH & Co. OG

properties.description: Das Hotel Stefanie ist das älteste Hotel Wiens und damit einer langen Tradition von Gastfreundschaft verpflichtet.
properties.name: Hotel Stefanie

properties.condition: fully restored building from late 19th century
properties.name: Zola Palais de Bohème

properties.description:en: House in the modernist style designed by the Austrian architect Paul Engelmann and the Austrian philosopher Ludwig Wittgenstein.. Currently the culutral institute of the Bulgarian Embassy.
properties.name: Haus Wittgenstein

properties.description:en: Building. "The house without eyebrows". One of the central buildings of the Viennese Modernism. It marks a departure from historicism, but also from the floral decor of secessionism.. (Source: Wikipedia:de)
properties.name: Looshaus

properties.artist_name: Johann Bernhard Fischer von Erlach;Mathias Rauchmiller
properties.name: Pestsäule

properties.description:en: Site of myth: A baker's apprentice here discovered a basilisk in the well and killed it with a mirror. (Source: Wikipedia:de)
properties.name: Basiliskenhaus

properties.description:en: 1990 building with notable and controversial architecture.
properties.name: Haas-Haus
Hotel Ananas
properties.note: The official address of this hotel building is Rechte Wienzeile 93-95 but since there's no entrance there and the hotel is quite big I decided to use the address of the parking entrance near the main entrance.

properties.description:en: Art Nouveau animated clock. On the hour, music plays and pairs of characters from Austrian history such as Empress Maria Theresa and composer Joseph Haydn appear.
properties.name: Ankeruhr

properties.description:en: The smallest house in Vienna. Two storeys, 14 sq.m. Occupied by a clock/goldsmith shop.
properties.name: Das kleinste Haus von Wien

properties.description:en: "Summer building" for Viennese merchant/banker Johan Geymüller, furnished with original furniture from the first half of the C19th. (Source: operator website)
properties.name: Geymüllerschlössel

properties.description:en: An unusual factory with a Persian facade in order to help market the "Persian powder" - an insect repellent made from dried pyrethrum flower. CURENTLY CLOSED. (Source: website)
properties.name: Zacherlfabrik

properties.description:en: Pavilion designed for 1958 Brussels World Expo. Now exhibition space for Austrian art in an international context, dating from 1945 to the present day.
properties.name: Belvedere 21

properties.description:en: Mozart and his family lived there from 1784 to 1787, during which time he wrote the world-famous opera “The Marriage of Figaro” and three of the six Haydn Quartets. (Source: museum website)
properties.name: Mozarthaus Vienna

properties.description:en: Local museum of the 19th district of Vienna of Döbling. (Source: Wkipedia:de)
properties.name: Bezirksmuseum Döbling

properties.description:en: 2.7 sq.km. of bars, pubs, clubs, restaurants
properties.name: Bermudadreieck

properties.description:en: Gives its name to the street due to a "beautiful llantern" hung here (now a replica, the origial is in Wien Museum). (Source: Wikipedia:de)
properties.name: Zur schönen Laterne

properties.description:en: Museum about heating - the heat as an issue of social and cultural changes. (Source: Wien government website)
properties.name: Brennpunkt°

properties.name: Spanische Hofreitschule

properties.description:en: Royal burial place. Since 1633, the the principal place of entombment for members of the House of Habsburg. The bones of 145 Habsburg royalty, plus urns containing the hearts or cremated remains of four others, are here, including 12 emperors and 18 empre
properties.name: Kaisergruft

properties.description:en: "Heroes Square" - Public space in front of Hofburg Palace. Many important actions and events took place here, most notably Adolf Hitler's ceremonial announcement of the Austrian Anschluss to Nazi Germany on 15 March 1938. (Source: wikipedia:en)
properties.name: Heldenplatz

properties.description:en: Bank Austria Art Collection. Around 10,000 works and one of foremost collections of Austrian art. Begins in the late C19th with historicism and Viennese art nouveau. Central focus on post-1945 avant-garde painting and contemporary art. (Source: website)
